State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5901            322-6441              322-6444

January 30, 1985

Peter M. Barmack
Covington & Crowe
P.O. Box 1515
Ontario, CA  91762

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our No. A-85-006

Dear Mr. Barmack:

     This letter is to confirm my previous telephone advice to you, rendered on January 23, 1985.

FACTS

     You represent a Major Donor Committee (MD), R. E. Wolfe Enterprises of California, which, together with its parent entity, Tri-City Construction Co. of Kansas City, filed a consolidated major donor statement (Form 461).  MD became a guarantor on a loan from a commercial bank to a borrower who, in turn, loaned the funds to a candidate.  The candidate is now unable to repay the loan to the borrower, who is, in turn, unable to repay the bank.  Consequently, MD has been making payments to the bank as guarantor on the loan.  MD knew the purpose of the loan at the time it was taken out and guaranteed by MD.

QUESTION

     What reporting responsibilities are there on the part of MD?

CONCLUSION

     MD must report the payments which it actually has made to the bank as contributions to the candidate.  The candidate must report these as contributions from MD.

Peter M. Barmack
January 30, 1985
Page 2


ANALYSIS

The Political Reform Act/ defines the term
"contribution" as follows:

"Contribution" means a payment, a forgiveness of a
loan, a payment of a loan by a third party, or an
enforceable promise to make a payment except to the
extent that full and adequate consideration is
received unless it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political
purposes....  (Emphasis added.)

Section 82015.

In the instant situation we have "a payment of a loan by a
third party" for which "full and adequate consideration" has not
been received.  Given the surrounding circumstances, it is clear
that MD knew the loan was to be used for political purposes.  MD
knew that the loan it was guaranteeing was to be used to make a
loan to the candidate.

The payments on the loan on behalf of the borrower which
are made by MD then become "contributions" as they are made and
should be reported on the appropriate reports.

If you have any further questions regarding this advice, I
may be reached at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,


Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

Ontario, CA  91762

                                    Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                         Our No. A-85-006

Dear Mr. Barmack:

     This letter is to confirm my previous telephone advice to
you, rendered on January 23, 1985.

                              FACTS

     You represent a Major Donor Committee (MD), R. E. Wolfe
Enterprises of California, which, together with its parent
entity, Tri-City Construction Co. of Kansas City, files a
consolidated major donor statement (Form 461).  MD became a
guarantor on a loan from a commercial bank to a borrower who, in
turn, loaned the funds to a candidate.  The candidate is now
unable to repay the loan to the borrower, who is, in turn,
unable to repay the bank.  Consequently, MD has been making
payments to the bank as guarantor on the loan.  MD knew the
purpose of the loan at the time it was taken out and guaranteed
by MD.

                             QUESTION

     What reporting responsibilities are there on the part of
MD?

                            CONCLUSION

     MD must report the payments which it actually has made to
the bank as contributions to the candidate.  The candidate must
report these as contributions from MD.

Peter M. Barmack
January 30, 1985
Page 2


ANALYSIS

The Political Reform Act1/ defines the term
"contribution" as follows:

"Contribution" means a payment, a forgiveness of a
loan, a payment of a loan by a third party, or an
enforceable promise to make a payment except to the
extent that full and adequate consideration is
received unless it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political
purposes....  (Emphasis added.)

Section 82015.

In the instant situation we have "a payment of a loan by a
third party" for which "full and adequate consideration" has not
been received. Given the surrounding circumstances, it is clear
that MD knew the loan was to be used for political purposes. MD
knew that the loan it was guaranteeing was to be used to make a
loan to the candidate.

The payments on the loan on behalf of the borrower which
are made by MD then become "contributions" as they are made and
should be reported on the appropriate reports.

If you have any further questions regarding this advice, I
may be reached at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,


Robert E. Leigh
Counsel
Legal Division

1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code.

COVINGTON & CROWE
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
1131 WEST SIXTH STREET
POST OFFICE BOX 1515
ONTARIO, CALIFORNIA 91762

MAURICE G. COVINGTON
HAROLD J. BALLIN
SAMUEL [illegible]
GEORGE [illegible] 11 30 All U3
ROBERT E. DOUGHERTY
DONALD G. HASLAM
ROBERT F. SCHAUER
EDWARD A. HOPSON
JAMES R. SMITH
STEPHEN R. WADE
JETTE R. ANDERSON
AUDREY A. PERRI
RANDALL J. PITRE
PETER M. BARMACK
LESLIE O. ADMIRE

TELEPHONE
(714) 983-9393

January 10, 1985                    EXPRESS DELIVERY


FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California
95814

Attn: Ms. Carla Wardlow

Re  :  Request for Advice

Dear Ms. Wardlow:

     This office on behalf of a major donor committee, requests
the advice of the FPPC as follows:

     FACTS

     Major Donor Committee (MD) is a guarantor on a loan made by
a bank to borrower (B), the proceeds of which loan were thereafter
loaned by (B) to a candidate for public office (C).  MD did not
guarantee the loan between B and C.  B has not made payments to
the bank on the loan between B and the bank, requiring that payments
be made, as guarantor, by MD.  MD does not know the status of the
loan between B and C, and has not made any payments directly to
either B or C.

     ADVICE REQUESTED

     We respectfully request your advice on whether or not MD has
any obligation to report the payments made to the bank, as guaran-
tor, on behalf of B.  If so, what is the authority and basis for
said obligation?  Lastly, if there is an obligation, to report
these payments by MD, how should the transaction be reported on
form 461, Expenditure and Major Donor Campaign Statement?

     Your prompt response to enable us to meet the January 31, 1985
deadline for the Semi-Annual statement for the period ended December
31, 1984 is appreciated.

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Re: Request for Advice
January 10, 1985
Page 2


     Should you have any questions or need further information
in order to provide the requested advice, we request you contact
the undersigned immediately.

                                        Respectfully,

                                        COVINGTON & CROWE

                                        Peter M. Barrack
